mnemonics: Use the 'PAPER' test: Would you be okay if this data was printed on a lost piece of paper? If not, it needs protection.
keyPoints: Intuitive methods rely on common sense and practical judgment rather than complex technical knowledge.
They act as a first line of defense, encouraging a proactive security mindset for everyday situations.
These methods are highly effective for identifying basic privacy risks and preventing simple, human-error related data breaches.
commonMistakes: Over-relying on intuition and dismissing the need for formal security policies and technical controls.
Assuming a method is 'obvious' and therefore not properly training all staff, leading to inconsistent application.
